Both parts of the question are addressed, meeting the criteria for 'correct.'

questions.question: Look at paragraph 18. What happens as a result of people not having a common language and not knowing the area?

responses.content: (13) A cell phone comes out of a pocket. Within seconds, the rest of the world’s five billion phones follow. All of them—even those compatible with the region’s towers—are displaying some version of “NO SIGNAL”. The cell networks have all collapsed under the unprecedented load.
responses.bucket_rationale: The student's highlight is: "A cell phone comes out of a pocket. Within seconds, the rest of the world’s five billion phones follow. All of them—even those compatible with the region’s towers—are displaying some version of “NO SIGNAL”. The cell networks have all collapsed under the unprecedented load." After normalization, this matches exactly one of the pre-approved correct answers. The highlight is within the calculated character limit and directly shows a cause-and-effect relationship: the cause (everyone using their phones at once) leads to the effect (the cell networks collapse). Therefore, the response is correct.
